description_long: This object consists of two stylized trees with tall, cylindrical trunks that have a textured bark pattern resembling that of deciduous trees. The trunks are topped with large, faceted, roughly spherical canopies that are covered in a mottled green texture, simulating dense foliage. The canopies are slightly asymmetrical, with one tree being taller and having a larger canopy than the other. At the base of each tree, there are truncated conical stumps, possibly representing cut or fallen sections of trunk. The overall appearance is low-poly and stylized, suitable for urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas. The trees lack visible branches and have no exposed roots.
description: A pair of stylized, low-poly trees with tall, textured trunks and large, faceted green canopies, accompanied by truncated stumps at their bases.
description_view_0: Front view showing both trees with their canopies and all four stumps clearly visible, highlighting the height difference between the two trees.
description_view_1: Angled view emphasizing the side profiles of the trunks and canopies, with the stumps arranged in a staggered line.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the backs of the canopies and trunks, with the stumps partially obscured behind the trees.
description_view_3: Side view showing the relative positions of the trees and stumps, with the canopies overlapping and the stumps grouped to the right.
